programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Steve asks the FBI to protect Mark from bereaved syndicate members who blame him for the death of an organised-crime kingpin. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Scott Baio and Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan tries to find meaning in a murdered private eye's last words, while Jack and Steve vie for the attentions of the dead man's secretary. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble learns that his boss has recognised him, but wants to help, rather than turn him in

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry faces the unusual prospect of having to cross-examine a parrot, the only witness to the death of a wealthy man whose wife is accused of murdering him.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker finds himself with a serious challenge when a notorious killer is reported to be in Texas. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose methods are rooted in the Old West

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A practical joke turns into a murder mystery when a skeleton found on a tour bus turns out to be authentic. Mac and Stella are given the task of identifying the victim, but the evidence points to two periods in Times Square's history, 20 years apart. With Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: When fellow workers at a labour camp nickname Richard Kimble 'Doc', three criminals to come to him for help when a boy they kidnapped is hurt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Stone comes under pressure from both Schiff and members of the public as he prepares to prosecute the person suspected of bombing an abortion clinic

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team must ascertain whether the murder of a Navy officer, whose body was found on a rural road, was the result of a bar fight or because he knew too much about an attack on a fellow female officer. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and McGee begin a juice detox to purge their bodies of impurities. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

Resuming the persona, Callen goes in search of the gang, one of whom is killed in a drive-by shooting

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the case of a building worker who died in an underground explosion. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden examine the death of a teenager found strangled and floating in a creek

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

Deliberately obstructing his enquiries, she threatens to share her suspicions about him with the rest of the staff - leading to a tense confrontation in the car park. Simon faces domestic strife as his brother's wedding draws nearer, leading him into a heated argument with bride-to-be Claire. Drama, starring Christine Stephen-Daly, Zita Sattar and Dorian Healy

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Skase handles a delicate inquiry into an assault on a gay man _ but the case is complicated by the victim's reluctance to come forward

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nellie meets her arch enemy and is forced to face up to an unhappy past. Ruth bites off more than she can chew, and Mark does a spot of DIY

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nellie makes an important decision. Grant continues to make Sharon's life a misery. Mark gets on the wrong side of Ruth and Gita gives Sanjay a new sense of direction

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: The firefighters race against time to save the victims of the road traffic accident, but struggle to release the injured and secure the area before lives are lost

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: David takes on the local health authority, outraged that they won't perform a potentially life-saving heart-transplant operation on a patient suffering from Down's syndrome. While he's talking to the Press and generally doing his best to a rouse public feeling, wife Clare struggles to find a job, then discovers she's pregnant. Adrian Lukis and Fiona Gillies star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul is reluctantly drawn into a complicated crash investigation, becoming the target of a disturbed woman who turns up at the surgery baying for blood. Meanwhile, Al prepares for her wedding day - but it seems the dashing doctor may not make it to the church on time. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant, Tamzin Malleson and Tim Vincent

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon worries her biological clock is ticking away and decides the time has come to have a baby - much to the alarm of Tracey. Meanwhile, Dorien tries to hit the headlines with tales of her amazing conquests. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's new fixation over personal hygiene and laundry leads Diana to suspect he has a new girlfriend. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene is sent into action to photograph the invasion plans and prepares the long-distance duck for its flight to England. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Young-at-heart trio Compo, Clegg and Foggy gamely volunteer to investigate reports of something sinister stirring in the woods. Rural comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Radio quiz Up to You comes to Kembleford and the sleuth suspects one of the cast is responsible when the landlady of the house they are staying at is murdered. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of a Colonial Postal Service officer when the fair comes to Kembleford. Drama, starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack examines a case previously undertaken by a deceased colleague, who was convinced that the alleged suicide of a journalist was a cover-up by the American government. Enlisting the help of a UFO-spotter, the team discovers the reporter witnessed a mysterious air crash near a US Air Force base, and the retired officers try to track down the original personnel to get some answers. Guest starring Mark Williams, Glynis Barber, Michael Brandon and Raquel Cassidy, with Amanda Redman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Double bill. A suspected paedophile is found dead in a playground, so inexperienced detective Rory Drennan calls in Nikki and Jack to attend. It's not long before the team links his murder to the case of a missing young girl, so the child-protection officers are also brought in - though it soon becomes apparent the youngster's social worker blames herself for the disappearance. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern, with Claudie Blakley, Lorraine Burroughs and John Dagliesh

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Skase handles a delicate inquiry into an assault on a gay man _ but the case is complicated by the victim's reluctance to come forward

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: Lizzie must deal with her mother's bold defence of the York cause, while the appearance of a boy in Burgundy claiming to be a young prince threatens to further destabilise King Henry's rule. King Henry's mother Lady Margaret Beaufort faces the consequences of harbouring a dark secret. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bonfire night sees fireworks of a different kind in this one-hour special from 2000 which marked the departure of Frank Butcher following the discovery of his affair. As the Vic fills with drunken revellers for Peggy's party, Frank panics when he realises the note he left his wife has gone missing

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Guests Vanessa Williams, Mark Gatiss, Nikkie Tutorials and Denise Van Outen join Michelle for a special episode celebrating her 23rd wedding anniversary, in which we discover how lockdown has affected romance

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce explores issues of gender identity, getting in touch with her masculine side as she learns what it means to be non-binary. She also encourages some of her male friends to experiment with drag and questions how raising children with fixed gender ideas can affect their development in later life

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters visit a three-bedroom semi in Miles Green, Stoke, a two-bedroom maisonette in Bow, London, and a piece of land in Kings Heath, Birmingham, destined to be built upon

programme.title: Dom Does America

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and David Harper help two teams scour Edinburgh's Royal Highland Centre to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp hears about an ice-cream van that's been doing the rounds since 1936

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Despite romancing Helena, Ash is tempted to stray. Meanwhile, Pav and Mich nearly come to blows. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Science and technology experts Spencer Kelly, Dr Helen Lawal, Dallas Campbell, Ajay Tegala and Rory Cellan-Jones take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Welsh Borders with a retired couple of keen gardeners who have £500,000 to plough into buying a peaceful home in the country. While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Florist Lucy, personal trainer AJ and car sales executive Sophie compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth continue to scour the shops of South Wales for antiques, where one expert takes a punt on a piece of local history

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of caravan life on the Ards Peninsula in Northern Ireland, telling the stories of those who leave their problems behind in the city and escape to the seaside

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea repairs a memorial to First World War soldiers from the village of Norton, and a woman seeks help restoring a teddy bear that belonged to her late stepfather. Plus, Geoff Harvey gets to work on a German-built fruit machine and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a precious solid silver trophy presented by the Royal Navy

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: Cat and Liam solidify their relationship and Jake's dad finds out about his secret side hustle, while Liam, Callum and George finally land a new job when Wayne Lineker, owner of super club O Beach, asks them to make a number of videos promoting an event

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, contestants include twins from Hull, a professional face painter from Bristol and a prison officer from Preston

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering the contestants' first experience of a professional kitchen, recalling those who thrived and those who only just survived the intense pressure. They also revisit a semi-final challenge from 2013 in which Janet Street-Porter, Shane Lynch, Les Dennis, Speech Debelle, Brian Capron and Adrian Edmondson cooked a three-course meal for 150 students, parents and fellows at Cambridge University's Gonville and Caius College

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble see how koalas get ready to mate and how to outwit the cunning hyena. Jean Johansson takes part in training on the lake to save a man overboard

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An Axminster property that took four years to develop, a small office in Basingstoke crying out to be a house and a Birkenhead terrace that has three - or maybe four - bedrooms

programme.title: Dom Does America

While they're busy, presenter Charlie Ross pays a visit to a recording studio in Wales, Rockfields

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's self-doubt is addressed by an actor, and a missed deadline puts Kam under pressure

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Katharine Merry, Derek Redmond, Ryan Sidebottom, Paul Robinson and Peter Shilton form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with a newlywed couple who have a £625k budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Head housekeeper Grace, telemarketing managing director Pierre and vineyard manager Adderley spend a day doing each other's jobs before judging whose is the hardest

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the West Country, where Roo meets Exmoor's equine inhabitants and Angus bares all at an exclusive beach that can only be accessed by tunnel

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make the wearer's eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Michelle also shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on a phone

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Can Sex Offenders Change?
programme.desc: Becky Southworth, the daughter of a convicted sex offender, investigates the world of sex offender rehabilitation. Filming across the UK in anonymous locations, she talks to a number of men living in the community, asking what drove them to their horrific crimes, and finding out what treatment is available to stop them reoffending

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble return to Longleat House to follow the lives of the exotic animals at its safari park, beginning by looking at the impact of the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Three people compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs, beginning with sausage factory supervisor Mica, bingo host Rob and head gardener Dan

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes space in the writing garden for plants that will flower later in the season and plants out tender vegetables for late summer harvests. Cameras revisit Frances Tophill's allotment to join her as she demonstrates an unusual method of reducing weeds and also catch up with a gardener in Wales who has dedicated years to breeding some very special varieties of rhododendrons. Plus, Joe Swift heads to Devon to find out about the design and planting challenges faced when creating a coastal garden

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary following British wildlife through the seasons. Spring marks a series of beginnings, as trees explode with blossom and mornings fill with the chorus of birdsong. Long-tailed tits frantically build nests, a stoat mother hunts rabbits to feed her playful young, while in the oceans, seahorses sway to a graceful courtship dance. As spring becomes summer, guillemot chicks leap from their cliffs to begin life at sea. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents action from the latest fixtures, including Wigan Warriors v Leeds Rhinos and St Helens v Wakefield Trinity

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Niall Strawson and Ferne Corrigan

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah finds Leah and persuades her to return home to see her uncle, and Douglas continues with strenuous work at the mission despite being told to rest by his doctor

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter L. The Hairy Bikers cook a lasagne and Jamie Oliver bakes a lemon and lime cream tart

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Surgeon Gabriel Weston investigates the human body's ability to adapt for survival, meeting with individuals who push this aptitude to the absolute limit. She meets a man who injects himself with snake venom, a woman living with only half a brain, and the only man in the world to be completely cured of HIV

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents the documentary from the team that produced Nature's Weirdest Events, exploring another selection of bizarre stories from across the globe. From his base in Nevada, he uncovers the secret of a suspiciously fishy oil slick, reveals what is making a lemming very angry and finds out about a man who can see with his ears

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Hull, Caroline Hawley is delighted to find a painting by local artist Steven Dews, and Nick Hall is dazzled by some jewellery. Paul Martin visits the Wilberforce House Museum

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their general knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Lazuli tribe becomes entangled in a three-way conflict when new family the Commandos spends the night in Whiskers territory and arouses the anger of the clan, with tragic results

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which famous faces compete in the cross-country bargain-hunting challenge. Hayley Mills and her friend and Wild at Heart co-star Stephen Tompkinson set out on a trip around north-east England in classic cars, receiving expert guidance from Christina Trevanion and David Harper as they visit antique shops and emporiums to find items to sell at an auction in West Yorkshire. Hayley detours to hear the little-known story behind Earl Grey tea and Stephen visits the home of a famous amateur football team

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms in Scotland and the Lake District. In Armadale, a hill farmer prepares for one of Europe's biggest sheep sales, while in Haweswater, a couple take on the biggest gamble of their lives by moving to a larger farm 10 miles north. An Aberdeenshire couple who began farming four years ago plan to raise baby ostriches, and a Northumberland shepherd prepares for the National English Sheepdog trials, and new farmers hatch ostriches

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Documentary about the people living and working in Manchester's remarkable housing boom. A millionaire developer plans to transform the city's red light district into a fashionable residential quarter, as well as taking charge of organising a fundraising concert for a homelessness charity. In Salford, a single mother finds herself priced out of the area she grew up in, and must find a new home for her two young children

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jack Dee hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by observational comedian Seann Walsh and king of the one-liners Milton Jones

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The Trial of Alex Salmond
programme.desc: In March this year, Scotland's former First Minister Alex Salmond emerged from the High Court in Edinburgh cleared of 14 charges of sexual misconduct, with the verdict concluding one of the most dramatic trials Britain has seen. This programme sees Kirsty Wark travelling across Scotland as she follows the trial and interviews people who were present at key moments surrounding the political scandal, and looking back at Salmond's political career and the growing rift between him and Nicola Sturgeon

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades traces heroic stories from the Second World War and how they still resonate today, revisiting three fixes from the barn; a watch, swagger stick and belt

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter reveals the container potatoes she planted in the first episode, George Anderson harvests his cabbage and blackcurrants and Chris Beardshaw checks on the progress of his box blight battle and explains the art of cloud pruning

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The first in a special run of episodes celebrating 15 years of the show, as John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on the best moments and share memorable challenges. In this first edition, John and Gregg relive some of the best and worst food from the early rounds of the competition, and look back at the celebrities' first taste of the MasterChef kitchen. For some it's a triumph, but for others it can end in disaster. John and Gregg also revisit two of their favourite challenges, both of which involved taking the celebrities out of their comfort zone. First, it's back to 2012, when footballer Danny Mills, writer and comedian Emma Kennedy and TV presenter Michael Underwood were sent to a film set to cater for more than 100 cast and crew of drama New Tricks

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Lazuli tribe becomes entangled in a three-way conflict when new family the Commandos spends the night in Whiskers territory and arouses the anger of the clan, with tragic results

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti join the staff working in some of the most unusual hotels around the world, learning the tricks of the trade along the way. This time, Giles and Monica help out at The Torridon in the Scottish Highlands, Britain's most northerly five-star hotel. Its need to balance a luxury stay with being self-sufficient has Monica collecting seaweed for the kitchen and helping to herd Highland cattle, while Giles learns to change toilet seats and joins the staff in their afternoon off - for a swim in the beautiful but freezing-cold loch

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: The documentary follows British wildlife throughout summer, when animals flock from afar and competition for food is high. Hunters time their arrival from Africa to feast on the huge glut of flying insects, falcons chase after dragonflies, and sea urchins are forced to protect themselves from harmful UV rays. Plus, thermal cameras reveal the activities of nightjars on a Dorset heath and thunderstorms bring out hordes of hungry snails. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Susan Calman, in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Martin Hughes-Games and Lindsey Chapman

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth and Douglas disagree over how to raise David, Richard prepares a hotel suite for Regina to get used to her new-found freedom and Ed tells Anna he will publish her novel

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical journey, this time looking at dishes linked by the letter Q. The Little Paris Kitchen's Rachel Khoo cooks quiche Lorraine and Raymond Blanc demonstrates how to make a quail egg ravioli

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Surgeon Gabriel Weston investigates the most complex and mysterious part of the human body, the brain, meeting with extraordinary people with seemingly superhuman abilities. Dr Weston meets Derek, a man who was suddenly able to play piano after a head injury, and Paul, who has a hereditary genetic condition which means he can't feel pain. This episode also explores the recent developments in technology that allow doctors to identify if patients who they believe to be in a vegetative state are in fact conscious and aware

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents the documentary from the team that produced Nature's Weirdest Events, exploring bizarre stories from across the globe. From his base in Nevada, he investigates a man who gets drunk without touching a drop of alcohol and chart-topping molecular music

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston upon Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Nick Hall hunt for objects to take to auction. Presenter Paul Martin heads to Sewerby Hall in Bridlington to learn the life story of Hull-born Amy Johnson, the first woman to fly solo from England to Australia

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower decides it is time to move because the Whiskers have been at the same burrow for five weeks. Her pregnancy makes her bad-tempered, which puts her three daughters Kinkajou, Daisy and Mozart on alert and at risk of eviction from the troop

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Brookside veterans Ricky Tomlinson and Michael Starke embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge, kicking off in Cheshire and heading for auction in Southport. They are joined by experts Catherine Southon and Margie Cooper, and gamble buys include antiques from Japan and China

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Emily is brutally punished after Lydia catches her trying to escape, while Margaret is thrilled when Lord Fallon shows interest in Lucy, and Amelia's new friendship with Violet blossoms. Charlotte is also on the hunt for a new keeper and is determined to leave Howard, but events take a horrifying turn when Haxby discovers her intentions

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Margaret is delighted when Howard offers to be Lucy's keeper, Fanny makes an announcement that could threaten her position in the house, while Nancy is determined to keep Emily Lacey out of Lydia's clutches

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels to Tiree, the most westerly island in the Inner Hebrides of Scotland, which is one of the stormiest places in Europe with gales blowing 160 days of the year

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Documentary about the people living and working in Manchester's remarkable housing boom. A millionaire developer plans to transform the city's red light district into a fashionable residential quarter, as well as taking charge of organising a fundraising concert for a homelessness charity. In Salford, a single mother finds herself priced out of the area she grew up in, and must find a new home for her two young children

programme.title: Surviving the Virus: My Brother & Me
programme.desc: Documentary following doctors and twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken as they experience the professional and personal implications of the Covid-19 pandemic. In the third week of lockdown in the UK, Chris finds himself returning to work on the wards for the first time in ten years. Chris gets to work treating patients on the infectious disease wards, and he quickly learns just how unpredictable the virus can be. Meanwhile back in March 2020, Xand contracts Covid-19, which leaves him tired and sluggish, and he also develops an irregular heartbeat, which he suspects is part of the aftermath of his infection

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The repairs of a cash register, a camera and a calculator, three pre-digital items that helped shape the modern computerised world people take for granted today

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges a sculptor, a ceramicist and a painter to create something to celebrate a couple's 25th wedding anniversary, on a budget of £800

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble learn of the efforts of Longleat's keepers in the Australian bushfire animal rescue, and find out how to assess the health of an elephant from its poo

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Florist Lucy, personal trainer AJ and car sales executive Sophie compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower decides it is time to move because the Whiskers have been at the same burrow for five weeks. Her pregnancy makes her bad-tempered, which puts her three daughters Kinkajou, Daisy and Mozart on alert and at risk of eviction from the troop

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a 1936 edition of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network, beginning an emotional rail journey which takes him deep into his family's past. In the golden city of Salamanca in northwestern Spain, Michael visits the University to hear of opposition to the fascist takeover of Spain by General Francisco Franco, and gains access to the General's archive of enemies of the state. Travelling onto the capital city of Madrid, Michael hears how the bombing of a small town in the Basque region in 1937 inspired one of the 20th century's most shocking works of art, before making his way to the town of Huesca. Michael's final stop is on the border with France at Canfranc station, where he learns of the role the old terminus played in the evacuation of Jews during the Second World War

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: The documentary follows wildlife throughout autumn, when the fading sun brings a change of energy to the landscape, while creatures prepare for shorter, darker days. Sika deer stags battle for supremacy, dormice curl up for their winter snooze, barnacle geese arrive from the Arctic and baby seals face the tempestuous waves, while salmon complete their epic journey to spawn before they die. By December, birds huddle together as the first snowflakes fall, and the seasonal cycle begins again. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant has to use judgement and tactics to decide which other player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Steve Leonard and Christy Harrison for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah learns from Leah that Regina is back in Inverness and heads over to her farm with Roy Brigg's rifle. George also visits his wife and she attempts to convince him that she is a reformed character giving him a letter revealing events that his family has kept from him. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey through dishes prepared by an array of TV chefs, beginning with recipes and ingredients linked by the letter A. Highlights include Jamie Oliver's savoury apple crumble, and Sophie Dahl's toffee apple crumble

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston continues to unlock the secrets of the human body, with cases including a girl with two hearts, a man who can sing two notes at once, a woman who can bend in amazing ways and a man who can run 350 miles without stopping

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents the documentary from the team that produced Nature's Weirdest Events, exploring bizarre stories from across the globe. Here he unearths a whirlwind wedding ceremony, some exploding rocks and a woman with a twin inside her brain

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston upon Hull, Caroline Hawley uncovers a remarkable collection of suffragette postcards and David Harper is delighted by some Hull Daily Mail newspaper printing blocks, saved from the tip. Presented by Paul Martin

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart is forced to seek support from the Whiskers clan or risk losing the pups. Meanwhile, Flower has her own new litter, and a well-timed rainstorm ensures there will be plenty of food for the meerkats. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Celebrity friends Chesney Hawkes and Debbie McGee scour the south east in search of all things antique, accompanied by experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson. Debbie hopes to conjure up a profit with a mantle clock and a hunting flask, while Chesney is taken by a 100-year-old weathervane

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley takes the contestants to her favourite dancefloor as they go backstage at Strictly Come Dancing. It's Movie Week, so they must each deliver two looks based on an allocated movie, while also embodying Strictly's brand of lashes, glamour and glitter. The best have the opportunity to work with make-up designer Lisa Armstrong on the live show. The next task sees the hopefuls transforming their models into a superhero, with the worst two challenged to produce a perfect glitter lip

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. First up are John Bishop, Sarah Millican, Phil Wang, Ellie Taylor, Ed Byrne, Angela Barnes, Gary Delaney, Tom Stade and Laura Lexx giving their thoughts on relationships

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After advertising her home on Airbnb, Mandy takes in a guest from Russia - and they soon become more than friends. But can he be trusted? Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Tony Way (After Life)

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Lola has not had a customer in her beauty salon for two days so Mandy suggests she introduce fish pedicures. Only to buy the wrong kind of fish. Sean Lock guest stars in the comedy, with Diane Morgan and Michelle Greenidge

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: Lee Mack's put-upon anti-hero struggles his way through yet another farcical 30 minutes of his life in the real-life sitcom. Tonight, Stuart and his extended family take a ferry trip to scatter the ashes of a loved one at sea. However, when the hapless hero goes looking for his diarrhoea medication in his car, he discovers a stowaway, some class A drugs and some pretty explicit pornography prohibited by maritime law. Why does it always happen to him? Co-starring Patrick Baladi, Frances Barber, Clive Russell and Samantha Spiro

programme.title: Squeamish About…
programme.desc: Spoof documentary in which reporter Michael Squeamish (Matt Berry) takes a whistle-stop tour through the history of London, exploring everything from the Great Fire and the Blitz to jellied eels and Howard Westend, after whom the West End is named

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights from the opening day at Royal Troon in South Ayrshire, where Hinako Shibuno of Japan was the reigning champion

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades examines the evolution of modern-day transport through archive footage and revisiting three repairs from the barn - a Penny Farthing bike, a tram seat and a speedometer

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley joins a roller derby team ahead of a match, and Ashley John-Baptiste discovers how pushing yourself physically can lead to an increase in mental resilience

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The gravedigger and amateur cameraman profiles the area's countryside by filming its wildlife and people. He begins his exploration by trying to build a badger hide, from which he hopes to observe and record the animals' behaviour over the next 12 months

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a stream trickling through the depths of the forest

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: Tom Fort travels along the 92-mile-long A303 from the Hampshire town of Basingstoke to Honiton in Devon. Originally designed in the 1960s, the route has been the subject of many proposed upgrades - several of which were strongly opposed due to their potential impact on sites of historic and natural significance, including Stonehenge. The programme charts the highway's turbulent history, as well as examining the development of the region over the past 5,000 years

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: A morale-boosting stage show offers Captain Blackadder a route out of the trenches, but he soon finds his plans heading for disaster when Gen Melchett falls for the leading lady - Lt George. First World War comedy, with Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder applies to join the Royal Flying Corps, seeing it as a great way of getting out of the trenches and into a classroom. Comedy, guest starring Rik Mayall and Adrian Edmondson, with Rowan Atkinson and Tony Robinson

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's biggest white-collar heists, which saw a Malaysian wealth fund looted of $3.5billion and involved government corruption at the highest level, abuse of power and international money laundering. With little to go on, investigative reporters from the New York Times, Wall Street Journal and Hollywood Reporter retrace the dirty money, via real-estate deals and movie financing including The Wolf of Wall Street, back to the top echelons of the Malaysian government. The Prime Minister and his inner circle are implicated, assets are frozen, money is seized and the Malaysian people fight back

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Documentary in which writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith finds out what it took to survive and prosper in the most artistic and decadent royal courts in history. He begins with Richard II, who cultivated the first truly artistic court in England. Painters, sculptors, poets, tailors, weavers and builders flocked there to make their fortunes, but these were dangerous times, and getting close to Richard brought many to a sticky end. Featuring contributions by David Tennant and Clarissa Dickson-Wright

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a stream trickling through the depths of the forest

programme.title: Bunkers, Brutalism and Bloodymindedness: Concrete Poetry with Jonathan Meades
programme.desc: Jonathan Meades concludes a two-part documentary celebrating 20th-century Brutalist architecture, which typically takes the form of block-like concrete constructions. The presenter explores the post-war work of architect Le Corbusier, and argues that buildings once maligned by critics stood for optimism and grandeur

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The red deer's rutting season begins, providing the wildlife film-maker with the opportunity to seek out a stag he accidentally upset the previous year after getting a little too close for comfort. He also enlists the help of a friend in dealing with a medical problem that could affect his work

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a cold, dark winter night and a cosy little cabin on top of a snow-covered hill

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Monty travels to Japan in spring to explore different aspects of the nation's gardens. Amidst the cherry blossoms, he begins his journey by visiting the earliest surviving boating garden of the Heian period. He also looks at the rolling green moss of a Buddhist garden and learns the secrets of creating a Zen landscape before visiting an unconventional garden created by a modern design legend. The presenter also learns about the art of Japanese stonemasonry, the famous tea ceremonies and receives a lesson in the delicate art of traditional flower arranging

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: Michelle Dockery narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the east Asian country, beginning on the central island of Honshu. It is home to more than 100 million people, but most of it is mountainous and this wilderness is home to an astonishing range of wildlife, and people and nature are drawn together in the most unexpected ways

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: Historian James Fox explores the country's culture, beginning with the connections between Japanese culture and the natural world and the influence of the country's two major religions of Shintoism and Buddhism

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: 'Whispering' Bob Harris hosts a three-hour special, originally broadcast in 2018 to mark the 30th anniversary of the landmark BBC music show's last broadcast. Music comes courtesy of former Old Grey Whistle Test stars Peter Frampton, Richard Thompson and Albert Lee, and past presenters Annie Nightingale, Richard Williams, David Hepworth and Andy Kershaw all put in appearances, sharing stories from their time with the show

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a cold, dark winter night and a cosy little cabin on top of a snow-covered hill

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: Michelle Dockery narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the east Asian country, beginning on the central island of Honshu. It is home to more than 100 million people, but most of it is mountainous and this wilderness is home to an astonishing range of wildlife, and people and nature are drawn together in the most unexpected ways

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The gravedigger and amateur cameraman heads off to see the salmon jumping and films the round-up of the Exmoor ponies, one of England's rarest breeds. The annual event gets lively as the herd's owners, the Milton family, try to separate the mares and foals from the stallions

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a faraway mountain nestled in misty clouds and foothills, creating the scene using soft, brilliant colours

programme.title: Joanna Lumley in the Land of the Northern Lights
programme.desc: The actress travels up through Norway to Svalbard, the most northerly permanently inhabited place on Earth, where she experiences temperatures approaching minus 30C. Her aim is to fulfil a lifetime ambition to see the natural phenomenon, the Northern Lights. Along the way she explores the fjords of Lofoten, learns to ride a snowmobile and spends a night in a hotel made of ice

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at TV appearances made over the years by multi-award-winning actress Maggie Smith, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Afua Hirsch explores three African countries through their art, music and culture, beginning by charting Ethiopia's 3,000-year history

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Documentary exploring inherited knowledge and the meaning of tradition, craftsmanship and artistic processes in three African cultures, beginning with the weaving of a bamboo house in the remote highlands of southern Ethiopia by members of the Dorze minority ethnic group

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Dr James Fox takes viewers on a tour of some of the most profound artworks from Tate Britain's collection, including a self-portrait by 18th-century artist William Hogarth

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Art historian Janina Ramirez takes a virtual tour of the British Museum, whose galleries she has paced since she was a girl. Here she highlights objects as varied as an Aztec decorated skull, ancient Egyptian cat mummies and an 18th-century tea set

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford tells the story of how Dr Henry Gray and Dr Henry Carter produced academic textbook Gray's Anatomy - the world's most famous study of the human body. Based on dissections they performed themselves, the accuracy of its descriptions and the stark beauty of its illustrations made it an instant bestseller

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a faraway mountain nestled in misty clouds and foothills, creating the scene using soft, brilliant colours

programme.title: Joanna Lumley in the Land of the Northern Lights

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: It's a race to the toy store after Arthur realises he may have given away his chance at $2million. Who knew a defective swamp monster action figure could be so valuable?

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: The gang's efforts to help JJ master the ancient art of Blamjitsu are ruined by Walter's plans to capture the Beanotown Yeti. Dennis and the gang defend the Yeti, and help JJ unleash her Inner Blam, in a gravity-defying Blam-jitsu face-off with the WilburCorp Henchbot

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson

Having been chosen to join a top academy on the mainland he's preparing to move to Glasgow permanently - but not before playing in the most important annual match on the island.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A look at the life of the English king, who defeats the Viking by using cunning tactics and summoning the spirit of Ed Sheeran. Starring Tom Rosenthal

programme.title: The Pets Factor
programme.desc: Cat helps a cygnet who is in trouble after swallowing some fishing wire

programme.title: Mystic
programme.desc: Issie gets a new vision of Mystic running through the forest along an old railway track, and wonders if this might be the clue that will lead her to Blaze’s herd. After some encouragement, Issie decides to ride out on Blaze to find the tracks, and Caroline, Caleb, Dan and Natasha join her on the mission. As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they’re completely and utterly lost. With one of the gang’s future ambitions at stake, they face a race against time to get back to the stables. Despite their best efforts to hide what happened, the consequences of their mission have serious consequences for them all as Tom presents the group with a difficult dilemma. Who will be able to ride in the Cross-Country Trials?

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Series about the human body, exploring how it works and how it recovers when injured

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Medics Chris and Xand test a new theory to see if they can find out why skin goes wrinkly in the bath. Dr Chris meets a patient taking part in ground-breaking heart research, and tests the power of the eyes in a mind-bending trick

programme.title: Nine Minute Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky demonstrates how to make tessellating wallpaper and pupil-popping shades, and explains how to keep drawings in proportion

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Mr Malone holds a mock trial to resolve a dispute Martha and Ollie are having over the ownership of Edison, a cuddly lightbulb they won together at a fairground hoopla. Rufus is appointed Ollie’s ineffective lawyer. (His amenability is something his girlfriend Kat finds very frustrating). Lily is Martha’s disappointing lawyer - whilst promising an epic opening statement, Lily makes it all about Rob being in America. Ollie asks Martha to prove she can throw a hoop on target. Martha objects, but Judge Kat insists that it will be amusing to witness Martha throw hoops. Martha applies the scientific calculations she gave to Ollie to help him win Edison, but she still fails to throw a hoop successfully. Ollie nears victory when the court adjourns because Mr Malone is hungry. Later, whilst Rufus admits to Kat that he doesn’t feel he is cut out to be a lawyer, he offers Kat the largest slice of cake even though he wants it. This infuriates Kat. She tells Rufus that he needs to be less easy-goi

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: The Repair Shop

Plus, Geoff Harvey gets to work on a German-built fruit machine and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a precious solid silver trophy presented by the Royal Navy

programme.title: Full House
programme.desc: A slice of life at Judges bingo hall in Tonypandy, as families, couples and groups of friends are poised with their dabbers ready, hoping to land themselves the jackpot. But for club regulars it is not all about the winning. It's also a chance to get together and sort their lives out. Cameras eavesdrop on the likes of Debbie and her bingo-hating partner Eric, cash-strapped single mum Rose and her best friend Sophie, and tattoo-shop owner AJ, who has something he wants to share with his daughter and business partner Tammy

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: Cat and Liam solidify their relationship and Jake's dad finds out about his secret side hustle, while Liam, Callum and George finally land a new job when Wayne Lineker, owner of super club O Beach, asks them to make a number of videos promoting an event

In this episode, contestants include twins from Hull, a professional face painter from Bristol and a prison officer from Preston

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the Cambrian mountains, heading to the Tywi forest to look for goshawks. He tracks these birds of prey during the late part of the nesting season and finds fully grown chicks in a huge nest with bits of eaten prey below. Iolo also explores the Elan Valley to find the curlew, one of Wales' most critically endangered bird species, as they attempt to breed on rough ground near the reservoirs. And he discovers a stunning day-flying moth called the Welsh clearwing

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Can Sex Offenders Change?
programme.desc: Becky Southworth, the daughter of a convicted sex offender, investigates the world of sex offender rehabilitation. Filming across the UK in anonymous locations, she talks to a number of men living in the community, asking what drove them to their horrific crimes, and finding out what treatment is available to stop them reoffending

Cameras eavesdrop on the likes of Debbie and her bingo-hating partner Eric, cash-strapped single mum Rose and her best friend Sophie, and tattoo-shop owner AJ, who has something he wants to share with his daughter and business partner Tammy

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bonfire night sees fireworks of a different kind in this one-hour special from 2000 which marked the departure of Frank Butcher following the discovery of his affair. As the Vic fills with drunken revellers for Peggy's party, Frank panics when he realises the note he left his wife has gone missing

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Guests Vanessa Williams, Mark Gatiss, Nikkie Tutorials and Denise Van Outen join Michelle for a special episode celebrating her 23rd wedding anniversary, in which we discover how lockdown has affected romance

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce explores issues of gender identity, getting in touch with her masculine side as she learns what it means to be non-binary. She also encourages some of her male friends to experiment with drag and questions how raising children with fixed gender ideas can affect their development in later life

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack turns his comedic sights on how when the British fail at sport, it's done in a truly spectacular and glorious fashion. Taking in Wales' quest to reach a rugby World Cup final, Archie Gemmil scoring potentially Scotland's greatest ever goal and Derek Redmond's heart and hamstring-breaking appearance in Olympic history, Jack celebrates a talent for coming up just short and a love of those who gave it a really good try

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace footballer Jeffrey Schlupp, 2012 Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, Solheim Cup winner Bronte Law and Commonwealth champion swimmer Hannah Miley join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Despite romancing Helena, Ash is tempted to stray. Meanwhile, Pav and Mich nearly come to blows. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Science and technology experts Spencer Kelly, Dr Helen Lawal, Dallas Campbell, Ajay Tegala and Rory Cellan-Jones take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Welsh Borders with a retired couple of keen gardeners who have £500,000 to plough into buying a peaceful home in the country. While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Florist Lucy, personal trainer AJ and car sales executive Sophie compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth continue to scour the shops of South Wales for antiques, where one expert takes a punt on a piece of local history

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of caravan life on the Ards Peninsula in Northern Ireland, telling the stories of those who leave their problems behind in the city and escape to the seaside

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea repairs a memorial to First World War soldiers from the village of Norton, and a woman seeks help restoring a teddy bear that belonged to her late stepfather. Plus, Geoff Harvey gets to work on a German-built fruit machine and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a precious solid silver trophy presented by the Royal Navy

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver uncovers the story of Rob Roy MacGregor, who has for centuries been celebrated as a Highland maverick, as a well-intentioned rogue - Scotland's answer to Robin Hood

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: Ibiza Dreams
programme.desc: Cat and Liam solidify their relationship and Jake's dad finds out about his secret side hustle, while Liam, Callum and George finally land a new job when Wayne Lineker, owner of super club O Beach, asks them to make a number of videos promoting an event

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, contestants include twins from Hull, a professional face painter from Bristol and a prison officer from Preston

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering the contestants' first experience of a professional kitchen, recalling those who thrived and those who only just survived the intense pressure. They also revisit a semi-final challenge from 2013 in which Janet Street-Porter, Shane Lynch, Les Dennis, Speech Debelle, Brian Capron and Adrian Edmondson cooked a three-course meal for 150 students, parents and fellows at Cambridge University's Gonville and Caius College

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's self-doubt is addressed by an actor, and a missed deadline puts Kam under pressure

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Katharine Merry, Derek Redmond, Ryan Sidebottom, Paul Robinson and Peter Shilton form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with a newlywed couple who have a £625k budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Head housekeeper Grace, telemarketing managing director Pierre and vineyard manager Adderley spend a day doing each other's jobs before judging whose is the hardest

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the West Country, where Roo meets Exmoor's equine inhabitants and Angus bares all at an exclusive beach that can only be accessed by tunnel

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make the wearer's eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Michelle also shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on a phone

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Can Sex Offenders Change?
programme.desc: Becky Southworth, the daughter of a convicted sex offender, investigates the world of sex offender rehabilitation. Filming across the UK in anonymous locations, she talks to a number of men living in the community, asking what drove them to their horrific crimes, and finding out what treatment is available to stop them reoffending

programme.title: Reported Missing

Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch
