programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shane takes part in a charity race - but finds training for it hard work

programme.title: Luthaidh (Louie)
programme.desc: The artistic rabbit finds an old treasure map at the seaside

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: One of the animals sets off on an adventure in search of the words 'magnifying glass'

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon reads Am Fuamhaire Dìomhair aig Seanair, written by David Litchfield and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Tha Donnie Murdo agus Pamaidh ann an Ameireaga còmhla ri Donna Marie, aig àm Taingealachd. Fhad ’s a tha iad an Ameireaga, tha e tighinn am follais gu bheil an t-Ollamh Crògalach a-nis a sàs ann a Hàidreant. Aoighean sònraichte: tha Lindsey Russell ag aithris guth Henrietta agus tha Ben Willbond ag aithris guth an Ollaimh Chrògalaich. Danger Mouse and Penfold get a taste of Thanksgiving when they join Jeopardy Mouse in America. When in America with Jeopardy, they discover that Professor Ham Hands has joined Hydrant. Guest stars: Lindsey Russell as Hysteria and Ben Willbond as Professor Ham Hands

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Bouncing on the trampoline is the latest activity

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When the Riders uncover the location of Viggo's Dragon Auction, they must stop him from selling dragons and stealing all the gold in Berk

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Documentary following the activities of Wally, a two-week-old warthog who lives on the African savannah

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Archie and Ben are busy in the lambing shed, Rory goes in search of goose eggs and Morag and Neil are at the cattle sale

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: The history of the London rush hour, featuring montages of the city's traffic lights and the growth in the usage of cars

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Derek Knottenbelt operates on a horse with sarcoids, Fiona Crowden treats a cow that has rapidly lost weight, and Ealasaid Dick is in the midst of lambing

programme.title: Ever Believe Me - Sgeulachd Madeleine Smith (The Madeleine Smith Story)
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the 19th-century murder case involving Madeleine Smith, asking whether the Glasgow socialite really did poison her lover with arsenic. Presented by John Morrison

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: A fascinating look at the world of whistling, including a visit to La Gomera in the Canary Islands where the whistling language is taught in school

programme.title: Piping Live!
programme.desc: Alan MacDonald introduces performances by the Battlefield Band, Ross Miller and Charlie Stewart and Northumberland piper Chris Ormiston

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Hymns, psalms and personal faith stories. Presented by Iain MacKinnon. SIAN sing Glorious Things of Thee are Spoken, while Eilidh Eglinton explains the role faith has in her life

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Lon le Linda
programme.desc: Patrick and Heidi help Linda to make vegetable curry, and learn about how cauliflowers are grown

programme.title: Botannan Araid Uilleim (William's Wish Wellingtons)
programme.desc: Adventures for younger viewers, with the boy and his magic boots

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: When Hoppity is left in the garden, Flop and Bing have to go in search of him

programme.title: Òrain CBeebies
programme.desc: A selection of songs from Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh and the CBeebies ALBA presenters

programme.title: Na Floogals
programme.desc: While investigating boy hooman's project, the Floogals find themselves in a sticky situation

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Kim Carnie reads Dè an t-ainm a bheir mi air mo chat?, written by Simon Philip and Ella Bailey, and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall
programme.desc: Zora is preparing for an important competition, but something crops up that distracts her. Short Gaelic film for younger viewers

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Mamaidh Mùmam hires a servant, but the family find it hard to get used to her

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Fearchar plays netball

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to create ghostly slime and turn drinks cans into disco dancers. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Hillwalker Colin MacLeod investigates the history and origin of ancient Gaelic place names in the Kintail and Glen Affric regions

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about the polio vaccine, and how children have been taught the rules of the road

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: Things are busy in Stornoway for the fire fighters, the Loganair team and Dooley in the cafe. The Highland Gliding Club is hosting the first Scottish competition of the season, and Andrew Bruce is kept on the hop fuelling a range of aircraft in Wick

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Coverage of day three of the International Sheepdog Trial and the Supreme Championship. Presenters Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee introduce the best of the action from the climax of the competition

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: The day of Pàdruig's funeral sees the arrival of some unexpected guests, preventing the day from running as smoothly as it should. Màiri returns to the woods, determined to retrace her steps from that fateful night

programme.title: TradFest
programme.desc: Allan MacDonald presents music from TradFest Music Festival, 2018. Among the performers are the sisters Shelby Lynne and Allison Moorer, Nell Ní Chróinín and Ellen Potts

programme.title: Na Boireannaich a Thog Glasgow City
programme.desc: Documentary about Carol Anne Stewart and Laura Montgomery, the founders of Scotland's most successful women's football team

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: A big storm causes a lot of damage on the island of Struay, and Uncle Matthew's home is one of the buildings damaged

programme.title: Bruno
programme.desc: The playful monkey dresses up as a clown and juggles

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shane takes part in a charity race - but finds training for it hard work

programme.title: Luthaidh (Louie)
programme.desc: The artistic rabbit finds an old treasure map at the seaside

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: One of the animals sets off on an adventure in search of the words 'magnifying glass'

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Alec MacDonald reads Eanraig agus an Yeti, written by Russell Ayto and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Tha Donnie Murdo agus Pamaidh ann an Ameireaga còmhla ri Donna Marie, aig àm Taingealachd. Fhad ’s a tha iad an Ameireaga, tha e tighinn am follais gu bheil an t-Ollamh Crògalach a-nis a sàs ann a Hàidreant. Aoighean sònraichte: tha Lindsey Russell ag aithris guth Henrietta agus tha Ben Willbond ag aithris guth an Ollaimh Chrògalaich. Danger Mouse and Penfold get a taste of Thanksgiving when they join Jeopardy Mouse in America. When in America with Jeopardy, they discover that Professor Ham Hands has joined Hydrant. Guest stars: Lindsey Russell as Hysteria and Ben Willbond as Professor Ham Hands

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When the Riders uncover the location of Viggo's Dragon Auction, they must stop him from selling dragons and stealing all the gold in Berk

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Documentary following the activities of Peter, an emperor penguin who lives among 25,000 other penguins on the Ross Ice Shelf in Antarctica

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Archie and Ben are busy in the lambing shed, Rory goes in search of goose eggs and Morag and Neil are at the cattle sale

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary following student life, including a beer-drinking competition and protests

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Having made it through a very wet winter, Sweeny now focuses on new life on the croft as he heads into spring. It is one of his favourite times of the year, and it's all go as one of his sows goes into labour, but this is one that he has to keep a close eye on. He also deals with the challenges of lambing; a time of very little sleep and plenty of early starts. With over 50 already lambed he's half way there, but his early morning checks prove vital as he finds out when he needs to give help to one of his sheep which is struggling. This isn't the only sheep he has to help. Sweeny lives on one of the most rugged coastlines in Britain, and although it's beautiful it can be very dangerous for the animals as he knows all to well. He calls the local mountain rescue in to help one of his sheep distressed on the cliff edge

programme.title: Rose Reilly
programme.desc: Profile of the Ayrshire footballer, who became an icon for female players when she led the Italian team to victory in the 1984 women's world cup

programme.title: Eadar Chluich
programme.desc: Short comedy interpretation of Death of the Horse, sung by John 'Hoddan' MacDonald and starring Greg McHugh

programme.title: Annie Oakley
programme.desc: Documentary about the sharpshooter, who taught herself to hunt to support her family and became a celebrity as part of Buffalo Bill's Wild West show

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in remote and unspoilt lochs and rivers, this time visiting Loch Maree

programme.title: Air an Rathad (On the Road)
programme.desc: Donnie Mackay drives the latest Ford Mustang and meets fans of American classic cars. Linda MacLeod takes the latest Mini Clubman to Torridon to see whether it has come of age

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his den of dealers set up in Northampton. Karen Dalmeny meets her knight in shining armour, Jo Brayshaw hits the right notes, while Henry Nicholls is served up a right royal dilemma

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends from Wales answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as another four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic decides to move out of the surgery to give Louisa space, leaving Mrs Tishell concerned he will not be able to look after himself on his own. Elsewhere, PC Penhale helps Janice babysit, and Al is very concerned when he finds out about Bert's behaviour and hears there are bailiffs at the restaurant

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz, with six more contestants competing in Fastest Finger First to see who will sit in the hot seat and have the chance to take home the £1million jackpot. To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal

programme.title: The Lost Honour of Christopher Jefferies
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part drama based on the murder of landscape architect Joanna Yeates. The last thing Christopher Jefferies wants after being vilified by the national press is to return to the limelight. However, as it becomes apparent things won't be going back to normal any time soon, the retired teacher embarks on a fight to clear his name. Starring Jason Watkins, with Ben Caplan, Shaun Parkes, Nathalie Armin and Joe Sims

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Life & Times of Captain Sir Tom
programme.desc: A profile of the former British Army officer, who captured the hearts of the nation with his fundraising walk for the NHS at the height of the Covid-19 pandemic. With access to Tom's everyday life, the film explores the man behind the blazer and hears his story, including his time on the frontline during the Burma campaign in the Second World War. Tom also recalls his teenage years, previous jobs and nursing his wife through illness - as well as a near-fatal accident he suffered just 18 months ago

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of day one of the Ebor Festival, featuring the 3.15 Juddmonte Stakes and support races at 1.45, 2.15, 2.45 and 3.45. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick FitzGerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Chris, PJ, Nat and Andrea pit their wits against ruthless Chaser Shaun Wallace. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie reveals everything that's happened between him and Belle to Kim over the phone. When Nate arrives, ready to plead for his job, they're left wondering how much he overheard. Elsewhere, Vinny tells his father that they'll be able to subtly convince Mandy to get back together with him

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi has an accident, and an oblivious Seb thinks his mum has let him down as the twins prepare to leave for Australia. Meanwhile, Paul is disappointed with Billy over his 30th birthday plans, and Mary and Sean are shocked to discover a letter from Todd to Eileen demanding money

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. In 2019, the airport is having its busiest year to date, and are expecting more than 81 million passengers to fly in and out of the terminals, with its 76,000 staff ready to take on whatever is thrown at them

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: Narrator Sally Lindsay focuses on Coronation Street's legendary characters, beginning with its longest serving resident, Ken Barlow. There's a look at the best moments of William Roache's six decades in Weatherfield, from the show's first episode in 1960 right up until the present day. Chris Gascoyne (Peter) and Kate Ford (Tracy) reveal how William thrives on physical scenes, and the actor himself admits his passion for an on-screen punch up, and how he has always tried to make them look as real as possible

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: The duo try to escape their marriage troubles by investing in their future, but a disastrous business venture mires them in debt just as Bobby's playing career starts to wane. Bobby winds up managing a lowly non-league side, and Tina dismisses rumours he's having an affair - until her worst fears are confirmed. When he confesses to loving the woman involved, her world collapses along with her marriage and she finds she must start her life afresh. Drama, starring Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The Australian actor, singer and Superstar judge recalls his relationship with fellow ex-Neighbours star Kylie Minogue, which ended when she left him for rock musician Michael Hutchence. He also reflects on his battle to beat cocaine addiction and pays tribute to the woman who he believes saved his life

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from the second round of the campaign at Brands Hatch, featuring the fourth, fifth and sixth races of the season. With commentary from David Addison and Tim Harvey, and reports by Louise Goodman

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef makes a high-octane pit stop at the world-famous Sonoma Raceway. Nestled in amongst Sonoma's vineyards, James discovers the preparations for the IndyCar season finale and gets driven around the track by American motorsport hero Mario Andretti. James cooks trackside before visiting a new foodie Sonoma hotspot, simply called Shed. After a wholesome lunch made with only the best local ingredients, James gets invited to Mario's nearby vineyard for a glass of wine and to discuss the thrills and spills of motorsport

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice. Presented by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of day two of the Ebor Festival, featuring the 3.15 Yorkshire Oaks and support races at 1.45, 2.15, 2.45 and 3.45. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick FitzGerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Simon, Matt, Julie and Kelly pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at three of the soap's most-loved families, weaving its way through each family tree to reveal the loves and losses that the parents, sibiings and children have faced over five decades of drama. Up first are the Dingles, who first hit the screens in the mid-1990s. Led by bare-knuckle fighter Zak, they were not exactly nice people, but eventually they became popular with viewers and became the scheming family they are today. Gaynor Fay narrates this look back at the life of Zak, his three wives and his seven children

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: After the unprecedented double hit of coronavirus and lockdown, can local businesses and high streets survive? From barbers to bookshops, restaurants to fashion retailers - reporter Adam Shaw investigates are planning their bounce back - and hears from people keen to get back to work in an uncertain job market

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher's walk around the Wales Coast Path reaches Carmarthen Bay and the Gower Peninsula, an area known for its peaceful estuaries, sandy beaches and limestone cliffs. At Pendine Sands he hears the story of Wales' land-speed record holder and sees his original car roar up the beach once more. Sean also tries to beat the tide and reach the end of Worm's Head, the westerly tip of Gower

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. Tonight's edition celebrates a few British classics, including a family firm that has been churning out Stilton cheese for three generations. Cameras also go behind the scenes of Lea & Perrins to see the making of their world-famous Worcestershire Sauce, as well as coffee-roasting at Taylors of Harrogate

programme.title: Inside Asprey: Luxury by Royal Appointment
programme.desc: The daily workings of the high-end London designer, which has supplied jewellery to the royal family since the reign of Queen Victoria. Over one summer on New Bond Street, the sales team tempts VIP customers with one-of-a-kind, gem-encrusted handbags selling for an average price of £33,000, while in the workshop above, silversmiths are seen making much of Asprey's luxury merchandise by hand. The programme also highlights the challenge of crafting a three-piece jewellery suite to hit the shop floor on schedule

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university friends answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

Chris Gascoyne (Peter) and Kate Ford (Tracy) reveal how William thrives on physical scenes, and the actor himself admits his passion for an on-screen punch up, and how he has always tried to make them look as real as possible

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: The duo try to escape their marriage troubles by investing in their future, but a disastrous business venture mires them in debt just as Bobby's playing career starts to wane. Bobby winds up managing a lowly non-league side, and Tina dismisses rumours he's having an affair - until her worst fears are confirmed. When he confesses to loving the woman involved, her world collapses along with her marriage and she finds she must start her life afresh. Drama, starring Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The Australian actor, singer and Superstar judge recalls his relationship with fellow ex-Neighbours star Kylie Minogue, which ended when she left him for rock musician Michael Hutchence. He also reflects on his battle to beat cocaine addiction and pays tribute to the woman who he believes saved his life

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from the second round of the campaign at Brands Hatch, featuring the fourth, fifth and sixth races of the season. With commentary from David Addison and Tim Harvey, and reports by Louise Goodman

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef makes a high-octane pit stop at the world-famous Sonoma Raceway. Nestled in amongst Sonoma's vineyards, James discovers the preparations for the IndyCar season finale and gets driven around the track by American motorsport hero Mario Andretti. James cooks trackside before visiting a new foodie Sonoma hotspot, simply called Shed. After a wholesome lunch made with only the best local ingredients, James gets invited to Mario's nearby vineyard for a glass of wine and to discuss the thrills and spills of motorsport

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice. Presented by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of day two of the Ebor Festival, featuring the 3.15 Yorkshire Oaks and support races at 1.45, 2.15, 2.45 and 3.45. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick FitzGerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Simon, Matt, Julie and Kelly pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at three of the soap's most-loved families, weaving its way through each family tree to reveal the loves and losses that the parents, sibiings and children have faced over five decades of drama. Up first are the Dingles, who first hit the screens in the mid-1990s. Led by bare-knuckle fighter Zak, they were not exactly nice people, but eventually they became popular with viewers and became the scheming family they are today. Gaynor Fay narrates this look back at the life of Zak, his three wives and his seven children

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: After the unprecedented double hit of coronavirus and lockdown, can local businesses and high streets survive? From barbers to bookshops, restaurants to fashion retailers - reporter Adam Shaw investigates are planning their bounce back - and hears from people keen to get back to work in an uncertain job market

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher's walk around the Wales Coast Path reaches Carmarthen Bay and the Gower Peninsula, an area known for its peaceful estuaries, sandy beaches and limestone cliffs. At Pendine Sands he hears the story of Wales' land-speed record holder and sees his original car roar up the beach once more. Sean also tries to beat the tide and reach the end of Worm's Head, the westerly tip of Gower

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. Tonight's edition celebrates a few British classics, including a family firm that has been churning out Stilton cheese for three generations. Cameras also go behind the scenes of Lea & Perrins to see the making of their world-famous Worcestershire Sauce, as well as coffee-roasting at Taylors of Harrogate

programme.title: Inside Asprey: Luxury by Royal Appointment
programme.desc: The daily workings of the high-end London designer, which has supplied jewellery to the royal family since the reign of Queen Victoria. Over one summer on New Bond Street, the sales team tempts VIP customers with one-of-a-kind, gem-encrusted handbags selling for an average price of £33,000, while in the workshop above, silversmiths are seen making much of Asprey's luxury merchandise by hand. The programme also highlights the challenge of crafting a three-piece jewellery suite to hit the shop floor on schedule

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick faces a testing time when a terrified hospital patient claims her husband is trying to kill her, but Merton seems reluctant to get involved, while Vernon picks up a bargain all-purpose pest-control solution, enlisting David's help in his eradication mission. Gina's cousin Diane arrives to help her and Phil with the wedding preparations - much to the delight of PC Crane, who is instantly smitten. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Tricia Penrose and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Angus, a member of the Bleakridge Watch, a group of villagers who walk the streets reporting anyone who falls foul of the law, is found dead in his meat freezer. Barnaby and Winter meet Angus's devastated sister and her daughter and learn that Angus was investigating a recent spate of burglaries in the village, but had yet to identify the culprit. Angus' post-mortem reveals strange restraining injuries on his wrists and the plot thickens as Barnaby discovers just how far some people will go for power. Frances Barber guest stars as the leader of the Watch

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate a hospital department where three patients have died in suspicious circumstances in six months - the latest being a woman brought into A&E with flu-like symptoms. The detectives learn she died after being given a lethal cocktail of prescription drugs - but with an unsigned patient chart and an extremely busy evening on the ward, tracking down the killer proves challenging. Crime drama, guest starring James Fox, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel can't get Gill's remarks about her fling with Will out of her head, so ducks out of attending an awards ceremony with him, fearing their colleagues will believe she's sleeping her way to the top. He finally decides that dating her is too much like hard work and so announces they are no longer an item - a move that leaves Rachel gutted and regretting her behaviour. Janet isn't happy either - it's clear her daughter Elise is about to have her heart broken by Eleanor's son Ade. The duo are soon distracted from their woes by a new case involving the murder of a landlord and his wife who were shot at close range in their pub

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Comedy series. When a storm makes him homeless again, Wilfred has to find a new dwelling

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve is forced to cover for Phil when the father-to-be discovers Gina has gone into labour two months early and rushes to the hospital to help with the nerve-racking delivery. Meanwhile, Merton is excited when it appears a drug dealer could soon be brought to justice. Drama, starring Mark Jordon, Tricia Penrose and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack thinks that Andy has been stealing, Zoe tells Steve that Kim's body has been released and she will organise the funeral. Vic and Eric fight in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly is furious with Vic for his behaviour. Chris criticises Zoe's management of Home Farm. Terry has a secret meeting with Helen. Robert gets drunk and argues with Andy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike gets Tricia to sell the reject sweatshirts on the market. Liz breaks the news to Jim that his mother has died. Alec moves into number 12

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona goes to visit Steve, and Don pays Kevin and Tony their wages two weeks late. Alf and Audrey's meeting with the Germans is spoiled when the OAPs protest at the Town Hall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve is forced to cover for Phil when the father-to-be discovers Gina has gone into labour two months early and rushes to the hospital to help with the nerve-racking delivery. Meanwhile, Merton is excited when it appears a drug dealer could soon be brought to justice. Drama, starring Mark Jordon, Tricia Penrose and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a toddler goes missing from a high-street merry-go-round, his distraught mother reports him kidnapped and a massive police hunt is launched to track him down. However, with more than one suspect and no physical evidence, the search throws up more questions than answers - especially as the boy's mum seems to be hiding something. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet voices her concerns to Dodson about Gill's drinking, hoping it can be dealt with amicably, so she's unnerved when her observations are taken more seriously than she intended. Meanwhile, just as Rachel feels like she's getting to grips with the sergeant's job, she makes a fatal error that puts Janet and Chris in danger. The body of a man who disappeared 15 years previously is found on the moors. Inquiries lead to a nearby farm, where the detectives encounter something much more sinister than they could have anticipated

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid returns home from work to be greeted with Sally's shocking news. Family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. George, on the other hand, wants to maintain as low a profile as possible. First episode of the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny falls victim to conmen who have devised an elaborate loan and savings racket to fleece unwitting villagers. Meanwhile, Vernon, Bernie and David put their literary prowess to the test by entering a poetry competition run by the Ashfordly Gazette. Rural drama, starring Sarah Tansey and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic tells Donna she is moving back to the post office as he does not want her in the pub anymore. The Dingles arrive home to find the burger van thugs have graffitied their home

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon and Butch keep watch for the thugs returning. Jack and Sarah apologise to Andy for not believing him. Zoe visits Frank in prison and reassures him that he will be home soon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don decides to channel his drive into the garage to make it a success. Liz asks Fiona to keep visiting Steve, but his ex hates lying to Tony

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Jim she would like to buy him out of the house. Alf withdraws from the Mayfield Court fiasco. Maxine thinks Fiona is stupid for letting Steve muck up her relationship

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny falls victim to conmen who have devised an elaborate loan and savings racket to fleece unwitting villagers. Meanwhile, Vernon, Bernie and David put their literary prowess to the test by entering a poetry competition run by the Ashfordly Gazette. Rural drama, starring Sarah Tansey and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover a dead body surrounded by rabbits that have escaped from a local pet show, after someone deliberately freed them from their cages. At the crime scene, DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover that the victim died from a stab wound to the neck, and theorise that the murderer might be a competitor sabotaging the Belville Small Pet Show, and question the Best in Show defending champion Timothy Benson. The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A call girl is found murdered at an upmarket apartment, apparently the victim of a client who risks losing everything if his secret obsession with the woman is revealed. Leading the cross-examination, Phillips begins to suspect someone had a reason to frame the accused, and doggedly pursues the truth. Guest starring Greg Wise, with Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The discovery of slaves on the Pritchard farm has turned a murder inquiry into a multi-agency operation. The enormity of the case brings Dodson to Syndicate 9, keen to establish Gill's state of mind and efficacy as senior officer. Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Terry does not like the way Vera quizzes him about his private life. Don lays into Mike, accusing him of setting him up with a duff business

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is not allowed to see Frank at the police station, and he is formally charged with Kim's murder. Eric holds his after hours poker game in the wine bar

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank appears in court and Chris is sure he will get bail. Zoe is distraught when the judge delivers the verdict. Vic is pleased when Viv seems upset about his date with Kathy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina Ward makes a surprise return to Aidensfield and Bellamy encounters a key informant who might come in useful - but finds himself in trouble when the man disappears before he can offer a tip-off about an imminent armed robbery. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: While Angie's career in commercials goes from strength to strength, Ida fears that her son's marriage is growing weaker. Will Ida be able to stop herself interfering?

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.desc: Janet voices her concerns to Dodson about Gill's drinking, hoping it can be dealt with amicably, so she's unnerved when her observations are taken more seriously than she intended. Meanwhile, just as Rachel feels like she's getting to grips with the sergeant's job, she makes a fatal error that puts Janet and Chris in danger. The body of a man who disappeared 15 years previously is found on the moors. Inquiries lead to a nearby farm, where the detectives encounter something much more sinister than they could have anticipated

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike gets Tricia to sell the reject sweatshirts on the market. Liz breaks the news to Jim that his mother has died. Alec moves into number 12

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona goes to visit Steve, and Don pays Kevin and Tony their wages two weeks late. Alf and Audrey's meeting with the Germans is spoiled when the OAPs protest at the Town Hall

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack thinks that Andy has been stealing, Zoe tells Steve that Kim's body has been released and she will organise the funeral. Vic and Eric fight in the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly is furious with Vic for his behaviour. Chris criticises Zoe's management of Home Farm. Terry has a secret meeting with Helen. Robert gets drunk and argues with Andy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve is forced to cover for Phil when the father-to-be discovers Gina has gone into labour two months early and rushes to the hospital to help with the nerve-racking delivery. Meanwhile, Merton is excited when it appears a drug dealer could soon be brought to justice. Drama, starring Mark Jordon, Tricia Penrose and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid arranges a fishing weekend, blissfully unaware Jean's father is coming to stay. Classic family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid returns home from work to be greeted with Sally's shocking news. Family comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. George, on the other hand, wants to maintain as low a profile as possible. First episode of the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny falls victim to conmen who have devised an elaborate loan and savings racket to fleece unwitting villagers. Meanwhile, Vernon, Bernie and David put their literary prowess to the test by entering a poetry competition run by the Ashfordly Gazette. Rural drama, starring Sarah Tansey and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic tells Donna she is moving back to the post office as he does not want her in the pub anymore. The Dingles arrive home to find the burger van thugs have graffitied their home

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon and Butch keep watch for the thugs returning. Jack and Sarah apologise to Andy for not believing him. Zoe visits Frank in prison and reassures him that he will be home soon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don decides to channel his drive into the garage to make it a success. Liz asks Fiona to keep visiting Steve, but his ex hates lying to Tony

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tells Jim she would like to buy him out of the house. Alf withdraws from the Mayfield Court fiasco. Maxine thinks Fiona is stupid for letting Steve muck up her relationship

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny falls victim to conmen who have devised an elaborate loan and savings racket to fleece unwitting villagers. Meanwhile, Vernon, Bernie and David put their literary prowess to the test by entering a poetry competition run by the Ashfordly Gazette. Rural drama, starring Sarah Tansey and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover a dead body surrounded by rabbits that have escaped from a local pet show, after someone deliberately freed them from their cages. At the crime scene, DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover that the victim died from a stab wound to the neck, and theorise that the murderer might be a competitor sabotaging the Belville Small Pet Show, and question the Best in Show defending champion Timothy Benson. The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A call girl is found murdered at an upmarket apartment, apparently the victim of a client who risks losing everything if his secret obsession with the woman is revealed. Leading the cross-examination, Phillips begins to suspect someone had a reason to frame the accused, and doggedly pursues the truth. Guest starring Greg Wise, with Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The discovery of slaves on the Pritchard farm has turned a murder inquiry into a multi-agency operation. The enormity of the case brings Dodson to Syndicate 9, keen to establish Gill's state of mind and efficacy as senior officer. Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford sleuth investigates a double mystery connected to the local Greek community - the death of a restaurant chef and the disappearance of a baby - and wonders whether an academic event attended by some of the university's leading classical scholars could shed light on the case. Mystery, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Jan Harvey, Martin Jarvis and James Hazeldine

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A huge scandal threatens Bohemia, as Holmes is outwitted by a bewitching woman's blackmail who owns a compromising photograph of a European royal. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the brilliant Baker Street sleuth, with David Burke as his trusty companion Dr Watson, guest starring Gayle Hunnicutt

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A girl is followed home from a launderette and abducted. The man responsible seems to want Makepeace find him, which concerns Dempsey.

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur gets an illegal car phone, while Terry dates the wealthy daughter of a hamburger king who suddenly goes bankrupt. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Patrick Malahide and Peter Capaldi alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A former agent regresses to a time when he was captured and brain-washed by the KGB into killing four of his colleagues. After escaping from a mental hospital, he sets out to resume his mission, leaving Bodie and Doyle with the task of trying to protect them. Action drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two villains rob an art dealer of a valuable Goya painting, which they plan to sell in Amsterdam. Regan deduces the dealer's female assistant is an accomplice, and after leaning on her sets off to track down the duo. Seventies crime drama, starring John Thaw

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar visits Venice to save a damsel in distress - only to find himself plunged headlong into a seedy underworld of gangsters, intrigue and deadly revenge. Roger Moore plays the suave secret agent, with William Sylvester, Patrick Troughton and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Strange, seemingly malevolent encoded messages sour a wealthy man's wedded bliss, prompting him to seek Holmes's expertise. Victorian mystery, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A major South American drugs gang is set to break into the UK trade, but Dempsey's concentration is weakened by the reappearance of an ex-girlfriend from the States. Detective drama, starring Michael Brandon

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur makes a hasty agreement to stage a burglary after his tailor's staff go on strike demanding more pay. The bumbling safecracker he hires blows himself up, leaving Arthur and Terry with the task of liberating him from hospital before his bandages are removed and the duo can be linked with the botched crime

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a judge's death and finds a connection with a deadly terrorist group of young middle-class drop-outs that has declared war on the legal system. On catching one of its number, Bodie and Doyle learn of a bomb due to explode in a courthouse. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's informer Popeye is abducted after passing on information about a switch of some stolen diamonds. Working with Carter, the DI must catch the villains while protecting their source. Roy Kinnear guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five co-workers from a university finance department answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Former football manager Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort, offering an insight into this unique part of the British south coast peninsula. In the first episode, Harry is joined by Neil ‘Razor' Ruddock and Joe Pasquale. Neil is considering a move to the area, so Harry takes him and Joe to see a very desirable £5 million penthouse with some of the best views Sandbanks has to offer

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort. In this episode, Harry welcomes John Barnes and Chris Kamara, where they take to the water to see what the area has to offer from the sea. Harry challenges John and Chris to a powerboat showdown on the Solent, before they sample a local delicacy at Harry's favourite chip shop

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: The popularity of dashcams, mobiles and cameras mounted on cyclists' helmets means that many road accidents and near-misses across Britain are now being recorded. This documentary features real-life incidents captured by people at the centre of the action, including the man who could hold the world record for the tightest parallel park, and what happened when a man's runaway tractor crashed into his ex-wife's garden

programme.title: The Waterboy (1998)
programme.desc: An American football team's waterboy puts up with constant ridicule from the players. But one day his anger gets the better of him and he demonstrates an unexpected talent for tackling - so the coach gives him a chance to prove his worth on the pitch. However, he has to keep his blossoming sporting career a secret from his overprotective mother. Comedy, with Adam Sandler, Kathy Bates, Henry Winkler and Fairuza Balk

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: The LAPD finds itself on the hunt for a thief from a local jewellery heist. Even though the case may look fairly pedestrian from the outset, Riggs and Murtaugh soon get a reminder that all that glitters is not gold and suddenly this straightforward looking case begins to evolve into something deeper. Murtaugh is erroneously called 'grandpa' by another parent while at the playground, sending him into a spiral of insecurity. Riggs battles some serious demons after vowing to withdraw from his drinking habit and work on his sobriety. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo find themselves the target of a powerful diamond merchant, forcing Dempsey to enlist the aid of a former boxer he put behind bars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur must find a way of paying a £20,000 tax bill immediately, but fortunately Dave has a drastic solution - faking a drowning accident. Chisholm is suspicious as there is no body, while Arthur is spotted hiding out in a hotel. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Cowley and the team are confused by a chain of events that involves lethal narcotics, a series of killings and a drug addict. As they piece the clues together, Bodie and Doyle realise someone has a plan to flood the public water supply with lethal hallucenogenic drugs. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Golf: PGA Tour Highlights
programme.desc: The Wyndham Championship. Action from the tournament at Sedgefield Country Club in North Carolina, which was won by JT Poston last year

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp welcomes Nick Knowles and Rita Simons to Sandbanks, where they pay a visit to the nearby British Beach Polo Championships, and even get the chance to sit in a saddle. The following day, Harry visits his neighbour, celebrity interior designer Celia Sawyer, who gives him a tour of her harbour-front house

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp goes on a camping trip to Brownsea Island with his family, where he plans to catch and cook dinner for the whole family. After taking his grandson fishing, he heads for a cookery masterclass with head chef of the nearby Rick Stein restaurant. The next day, Harry and his son, Jamie Redknapp compete in a game of crazy golf

programme.title: Apollo 13 (1995)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. With Tom Hanks, Kevin Bacon, Bill Paxton, Ed Harris and Gary Sinise

programme.title: Ali (2001)
programme.desc: Biopic of champion boxer Muhammad Ali, covering events from his 1964 fight with Sonny Liston to his Rumble in the Jungle with George Foreman 10 years later. Directed by Michael Mann and starring Will Smith, Jamie Foxx, Jon Voight and Mario Van Peebles

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: TV presenter Fern Britton, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard, lingerie tycoon Michelle Mone and actor Neil Morrissey pit their general knowledge against a trivia expert as they try to win thousands of pounds for charity. programme.title: The Gauntlet (1977)
programme.desc: A downbeat cop sets out to escort a prostitute across America so she can give evidence in a high-profile Mob trial. However, corrupt officials are bent on ensuring the witness never makes it to court, and subject the pair to a series of attacks. Meanwhile, the mismatched duo's bid to reach their destination alive is complicated by a burgeoning romance. Action adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Sondra Locke, Bill McKinney and Pat Hingle

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a judge's death and finds a connection with a deadly terrorist group of young middle-class drop-outs that has declared war on the legal system. On catching one of its number, Bodie and Doyle learn of a bomb due to explode in a courthouse. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's informer Popeye is abducted after passing on information about a switch of some stolen diamonds. Working with Carter, the DI must catch the villains while protecting their source. Roy Kinnear guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five co-workers from a university finance department answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Former football manager Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort, offering an insight into this unique part of the British south coast peninsula. In the first episode, Harry is joined by Neil ‘Razor' Ruddock and Joe Pasquale. Neil is considering a move to the area, so Harry takes him and Joe to see a very desirable £5 million penthouse with some of the best views Sandbanks has to offer

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort. In this episode, Harry welcomes John Barnes and Chris Kamara, where they take to the water to see what the area has to offer from the sea. Harry challenges John and Chris to a powerboat showdown on the Solent, before they sample a local delicacy at Harry's favourite chip shop

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: The popularity of dashcams, mobiles and cameras mounted on cyclists' helmets means that many road accidents and near-misses across Britain are now being recorded. This documentary features real-life incidents captured by people at the centre of the action, including the man who could hold the world record for the tightest parallel park, and what happened when a man's runaway tractor crashed into his ex-wife's garden

programme.title: The Waterboy (1998)
programme.desc: An American football team's waterboy puts up with constant ridicule from the players. But one day his anger gets the better of him and he demonstrates an unexpected talent for tackling - so the coach gives him a chance to prove his worth on the pitch. However, he has to keep his blossoming sporting career a secret from his overprotective mother. Comedy, with Adam Sandler, Kathy Bates, Henry Winkler and Fairuza Balk

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: The LAPD finds itself on the hunt for a thief from a local jewellery heist. Even though the case may look fairly pedestrian from the outset, Riggs and Murtaugh soon get a reminder that all that glitters is not gold and suddenly this straightforward looking case begins to evolve into something deeper. Murtaugh is erroneously called 'grandpa' by another parent while at the playground, sending him into a spiral of insecurity. Riggs battles some serious demons after vowing to withdraw from his drinking habit and work on his sobriety. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.title: The Professionals

Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. The following day, Harry visits his neighbour, celebrity interior designer Celia Sawyer, who gives him a tour of her harbour-front house

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp goes on a camping trip to Brownsea Island with his family, where he plans to catch and cook dinner for the whole family. After taking his grandson fishing, he heads for a cookery masterclass with head chef of the nearby Rick Stein restaurant. The next day, Harry and his son, Jamie Redknapp compete in a game of crazy golf

programme.title: Apollo 13 (1995)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. With Tom Hanks, Kevin Bacon, Bill Paxton, Ed Harris and Gary Sinise

In the third and final week of the diet challenge, Michael explores the connection between comfort eating, and the different ways our brains respond when faced with a bar of chocolate as opposed to a pile of fruit and vegetables. He also has the unique experience of having a camera stuck up his nose to learn about the connection between weight, snoring and the life-limiting condition of sleep apnoea

Meanwhile, the creative team cast for a risqué film shoot for their annual Female Empowerment campaign for women of all shapes and sizes, and the designers and buyers head to sun-kissed Ibiza to spy on the latest fashion trends
